pattern: Free deletion turns a subarray-selection problem into picking one representative per distinct value, so only the sign of each distinct value matters.
hints: Since you may delete anything, any set of elements with distinct values can be collapsed into one contiguous block by deleting everything else.
So the real question is simply: which distinct values should you keep to maximize their sum?
Keep every distinct value that is positive and sum them; if no value is positive, you must still keep one element, so the answer is the largest single value in the array.

edge_inputs.description: every value negative forces keeping the least negative one
all zeros with no positive value present
duplicated positive and negative values collapse to one each
maximum length all identical positive value
mix where zero should be ignored, not subtracted

stress.description: random arrays across the full length and value range
